name: Lahnda (Western Punjabi macrolanguage)

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Lahnda
sources.notes: Macrolanguage article; no phonology is claimed here.

notes: MACROLANGUAGE ENTRY POINT, not a modelled G2P target. `lah` is the ISO 639-3 macrolanguage that BCP-47 macro-normalisation folds `pnb` (Western Punjabi) into, so without this file `pnb` resolves to nothing. The grapheme inventory is deliberately NOT specified here: the modelled variety is `pa-PK` (Western Punjabi in the Shahmukhi Perso-Arabic script), which carries the real inventory — use that code. No Glottolog code is recorded because ISO `lah` has no Glottolog languoid (it is a non-inclusive macrolanguage grouping, not a genealogical node); recording a family node instead would be wrong. Input contract, when a variety is modelled: Shahmukhi text (an Arabic-derived abjad; short vowels are usually unwritten), as documented on `pa-PK`.

ancestors.notes: Indo-Aryan — the classification node this language sits under.
